businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker transitioning into a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning robotaxis, humanoid robots, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is rapidly transforming from an EV manufacturer into an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform company. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B (-2.9%), with net income plunging 46.5%. Friday close $396.73 (-2.17%). Tomorrow (3/9) is the NHTSA FSD safety review deadline — 2.88M vehicles under investigation, 58 incidents linked. Over the weekend, Austin robotaxi base fare raised from $1 to $3.25, and Tokyo FSD Supervised public demos began. P/E 368.71x, Forward P/E 150.88x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack utility-scale storage
Services/Other: ~13% of revenue ($12.5B). Supercharger network, insurance, maintenance
FSD subscription: $99/month autonomous driving software (subscription-only since Feb 2026)
keyProducts: Cybertruck (base AWD trim with massive demand, deliveries pushed to 2027)
Optimus (humanoid robot, Fremont factory conversion underway)
Megapack (utility-scale energy storage, Megapack 3 and Mega Block launching 2026)

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme valuation. AI/robotics premium vs actual earnings

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects earnings recovery expectations but still very elevated

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: First-ever FY decline. Global EV competition intensifying

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Slight improvement QoQ but still historically low

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down from FY2024. Profitability recovery needed

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD Safety Review D-1 — Tomorrow (3/9) Final Deadline
growthDrivers.description: 2.88M vehicles under investigation with 58 linked incidents. Approval accelerates nationwide autonomous rollout; failure risks 30% downside. Final deadline after two extensions.

growthDrivers.title: Austin Robotaxi Base Fare Raised $1→$3.25
growthDrivers.description: Weekend fare increase reflects high demand (10-15 min wait times). Revenue per ride jumped from $6 to $8.25 for a 5-mile trip. Monetization signal.

growthDrivers.title: Tokyo FSD Supervised Public Demos Begin
growthDrivers.description: Tesla has started public demonstrations of FSD Supervised on Tokyo streets. First step toward Japan market entry and international FSD expansion.

growthDrivers.title: Phoenix, Miami, Las Vegas Robotaxi Expansion Planned
growthDrivers.description: Three additional cities targeted for mid-2026 expansion. Austin's 14 crash record is a hurdle, but scaling intent is clear.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader, though overtaken globally by BYD. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy, and 8.2B FSD miles is the core moat.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelming training data advantage over competitors.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is North America's EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian all depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 2025 global EV sales leader. Rapid European expansion, US limited by tariffs

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 SUV ($45,000) launching 2026, directly competing with Model Y

competitors.name: Xiaomi
competitors.detail: YU7 SUV outselling Model Y 2:1 in China

recentNews.headline: Austin robotaxi base fare raised from $1 to $3.25, demand management
recentNews.significance: Wait times averaging 10-15 minutes confirm high demand. Fare increase signals monetization intent

recentNews.headline: Tokyo FSD Supervised public demonstrations begin
recentNews.significance: First step toward Japan market entry and international FSD expansion

recentNews.headline: NHTSA FSD safety review D-1 — tomorrow (3/9) final deadline
recentNews.significance: 2.88M vehicles, 58 incidents, Austin robotaxi 14 crashes. Final deadline after two extensions

recentNews.headline: UK February sales plunge 37%; Toyota and Stellantis exit EU CO2 pool
recentNews.significance: Overall UK market grew 7.2%. European weakness broadening

recentNews.headline: Jobs shock and oil past $90 cap Wall Street's worst week since October
recentNews.significance: Nonfarm payrolls contracted, Iran war pushed oil to 2023 highs. TSLA -2.17%

analystOpinions.notableComment: 45 analysts, Hold consensus. Target $426.61 implies +7.5% upside. Tomorrow's NHTSA result is the key catalyst for target revisions. Tokyo FSD demo adds international expansion optionality.

risks.title: P/E 369x extreme valuation
risks.description: Massively overvalued vs current earnings. AI/robotics narrative failure risks severe correction.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD regulatory risk (D-1, tomorrow)
risks.description: 2.88M vehicles under investigation, 58 incidents. Austin robotaxi 14 crashes. Failure = FSD program suspension, 30% downside.

risks.title: European demand collapse — UK -37%, EU Jan registrations -17%
risks.description: UK, Netherlands, Denmark sharp declines. Toyota/Stellantis CO2 pool exit shrinks credit revenue.

risks.title: Macro headwinds — oil $90+, jobs shock
risks.description: Iran war + employment contraction could dampen consumer sentiment and EV purchases.

risks.title: CEO risk — X deal trial + political controversies
risks.description: Musk's legal and political issues continue to drive consumer backlash and brand damage.

buyReasons.title: 7.5% discount to consensus $427
buyReasons.rationale: Macro selloff overshoot. Holding above 200-day moving average support.

buyReasons.title: NHTSA pass tomorrow could trigger rally
buyReasons.rationale: FSD approval would unlock autonomous driving valuation re-rating.

buyReasons.title: Robotaxi fare increase — monetization signal
buyReasons.rationale: Austin base fare tripled to $3.25, confirming high demand and revenue potential.

buyReasons.title: Tokyo FSD demo — international expansion begins
buyReasons.rationale: Japan market entry makes global FSD expansion tangible.

overallOpinion: Tomorrow (Monday) is the final deadline for the U.S. auto safety regulator's self-driving review. With 2.88 million vehicles under investigation and 58 incidents linked to FSD, this is the defining moment: approval could spark a sharp rally, while failure could send the stock down 30%.

Over the weekend, Tesla raised Austin robotaxi fares by more than 3x (from $1 to $3.25 base fare), signaling strong demand with 10-15 minute wait times. The company also began public FSD demonstrations on Tokyo streets, marking its first step into the Japanese market.
UK February sales plunged 37%, oil is above $90, and the jobs report was a shock — macro headwinds remain heavy. The stock sits below both its 50-day average ($428) and 200-day average ($418).
Tomorrow's review result will determine everything. The weekend brought small positives — robotaxi fare hikes and Tokyo FSD demos — but waiting for Monday's outcome remains the prudent strategy.

sources.name: Electrek
sources.description: NHTSA FSD probe, 2.88M vehicles, 3/9 final deadline

sources.name: CNBC
sources.description: 3/6 macro analysis — jobs shock + oil surge

witty.text:
$TSLA (Mar 8) Self-driving exam tomorrow. Robotaxi fares tripled and Tokyo got FSD demos — Tesla studied hard. But will the regulator grade on a curve?

1/4
thread:
Austin fares went from $1 to $3.25. Demand is so high they raised prices 3x. That's either bullish or they're pricing out the early adopters.

2/4

Tokyo FSD demos are cool, but UK sales tanked 37%. The world can't agree on whether Tesla is the future or yesterday's news.

3/4

Target $427 (+7.5%). Buying before the exam is betting on the answer key. Wait for the grade.

4/4
